describe.lastDayOfMonth: the last day of the month
describe.lastWeekdayOfMonth: the last weekday of the month
describe.lastDayMinusN: {n} days before the last day of the month

parse.placeholder: Paste or type a Cron expression

warn.searchWindowExhausted: Only {found} of {requested} executions found in the next 4 years
warn.specialDroppedFromMode: Special character {token} is not supported in {mode} mode and was removed
warn.bothDayFieldsSet: Both day-of-month and day-of-week are set — expression fires when EITHER matches
warn.orSemanticsLost: Quartz cannot represent OR semantics across day fields — keeping day-of-week, setting day-of-month to '?'

errors.invalidStep: Step value must be positive, got {value}
errors.rangeReversed: Range start ({from}) is greater than end ({to}) in {field}
errors.specialNotAllowed: '{token}' is not allowed in {field} field for {mode} mode
errors.quartzNeedsQuestionMark: Quartz mode requires either day-of-month or day-of-week to be '?'
errors.quartzBothQuestionMarks: Day-of-month and day-of-week cannot both be '?'
errors.macroRebootUnsupported: @reboot is not supported (browser cannot detect system boot)
errors.weekdayOnNonDom: 'W' is only allowed in day-of-month field
errors.nthOnNonDow: '#' is only allowed in day-of-week field

descriptions.whatIs: A Cron expression is a string of fields that describes a schedule. Each field controls a unit of time (minute, hour, day, month, weekday). Originally from Unix cron, the syntax has been extended by Spring (adds seconds) and Quartz (adds seconds and year, plus special characters L, W, #).
descriptions.dst: When the active timezone observes DST, schedules within the spring-forward gap fire at the next valid time, and schedules within the fall-back overlap fire at the first occurrence. Switch to UTC to avoid DST entirely.
